question: Spread: Ceará SC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Ceará SC" if Ceará SC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "América FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: América FC vs. Ceará SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Serie B game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: América-MG holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their Série B home clash against struggling Ceará, fueled by six straight unbeaten matches at Independência Stadium and a defense conceding just 0.8 goals per game there. Ceará's dismal away record—winless in seven road trips with three losses in a row—amplifies the gap, alongside their 17th-place slide after dropping points in recent draws. No major injuries reported for América per official updates, while Ceará misses midfielder Jorge Recalde (suspended). Head-to-head favors América with three wins in the last five meetings, though Ceará's counterattack desperation could spark an upset in this mid-table survival scrap.

events.title: NEC vs. Feyenoord Rotterdam
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Sunday, April 12, 2026 between NEC and Feyenoord Rotterdam.

eventMetadata.context_description: NEC Nijmegen's solid home form at Goffertstadion, where they've lost just once in their last six Eredivisie matches, has traders pricing a tight contest against Feyenoord Rotterdam, whose potent attack led by Santiago Giménez faces vulnerability on the road with only two wins in the last five away games. Head-to-head history shows three draws in the last five meetings, underscoring defensive resilience on both sides and fueling the near-even implied probabilities split between Feyenoord victory, draw, and NEC upset. Momentum from NEC's midweek cup progress adds intrigue to this Eredivisie clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers hold the edge in trader consensus for their Pacific Division clash with the Los Angeles Kings, with implied probabilities around 60% reflecting superior recent form and offensive firepower led by Connor McDavid's 15 points over the last five games. The Oilers have won four of the past five head-to-head meetings, including a 6-3 rout on October 16, bolstered by goaltender Stuart Skinner's .915 save percentage in those contests. No major injuries reported on official lists, though Kings forward Adrian Kempe remains day-to-day with an upper-body issue, potentially weakening their top line. Home-ice advantage at Crypto.com Arena for LA adds intrigue, but Edmonton's road record (6-2-1) tempers that factor amid a grueling schedule ahead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes enter as clear trader favorites against the Utah Hockey Club, reflecting their elite 14-4-2 record and league-leading power play efficiency amid Utah's early-season struggles at 6-11-2. Recent developments tilt further toward Carolina: goaltender Frederik Andersen's return bolsters their .925 save percentage, while Utah grapples with key injuries to forwards Clayton Keller (day-to-day, upper body) and Logan Cooley (questionable). Head-to-head history favors Carolina's speed and forecheck, though Utah's home-ice desperation in Salt Lake City adds upset potential. Traders price Hurricanes at 65% implied win probability, factoring rest advantage after Utah's back-to-back and Carolina's road warrior form (7-2-1 away). Momentum and depth position Hurricanes to dictate pace in this Central-East clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues hold a strong edge in trader consensus for their Central Division clash with the Chicago Blackhawks, fueled by a recent four-game win streak and elite goaltending from Jordan Binnington, who's posted a .925 save percentage over his last five starts. The Hawks struggle defensively, ranking 28th in goals against per game, compounded by rookie sensation Connor Bedard's nagging upper-body injury status per the latest report, potentially sidelining their top scorer. Blues forward Robert Thomas returns from a minor tweak, bolstering their attack, while Chicago's road woes (1-5-1 away) and back-to-back schedule add pressure. Historical head-to-head favors St. Louis at home, though Blackhawks' power play could spark an upset if rested.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Kraken hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Calgary Flames, driven by superior recent form with five wins in their last seven games and a stingy defense allowing just 2.3 goals per game. The Flames, mired in a three-game skid, face roster challenges including forward Andrew Mangiapane's upper-body injury sidelining him, per official reports, weakening their top-six scoring punch. Kraken's home-ice advantage at Climate Pledge Arena adds momentum, where they've posted a 6-2-1 record this season. Head-to-head, Seattle dominated their October 26 meeting 3-1. Watch Flames goaltender Dustin Wolf's bounce-back potential versus Kraken netminder Joey Daccord's .915 save percentage, alongside Calgary's road fatigue from a back-to-back schedule.

eventMetadata.context_description: Winnipeg Jets' elite goaltending from Vezina frontrunner Connor Hellebuyck anchors trader consensus favoring them at home against the Philadelphia Flyers, with implied probabilities reflecting the Jets' 6-2-0 start and league-best 2.25 goals-against average. The Flyers (2-4-1) struggle defensively, allowing 3.86 goals per game amid injuries to forwards like Cam Atkinson (upper body, out) and question marks on rookie Matvei Michkov's status post-scratches. Recent Jets momentum includes three straight wins with Hellebuyck posting a .952 save percentage, while Philly's road woes (0-3-1 away) and back-to-back schedule amplify upset risk, though their power play (22.6%) offers counterpunch potential in this Central-Metro clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers enter their matchup against the Toronto Maple Leafs as defending Stanley Cup champions, fueling trader consensus with roughly 60% implied probability on Florida due to superior depth, playoff experience, and Sergei Bobrovsky's elite goaltending (.915 save percentage last postseason). Recent developments include the Panthers' strong 4-1-0 start amid minor injuries to depth players like Aaron Ekblad (day-to-day), while Toronto (3-2-1) integrates new coach Craig Berube's structured defense but grapples with goaltending instability—Joseph Woll's .890 save rate raises concerns. Key edges: Panthers' road prowess (championship run) versus Leafs' home scoring (Matthews' 5 goals), with rivalry intensity and power-play efficiency (Florida 25%, Toronto 22%) pivotal; watch pre-game lines for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Montreal Canadiens hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets, driven by Columbus's ongoing defensive struggles and key absences. The Blue Jackets enter on a four-game skid, surrendering 4.5 goals per contest amid injuries to top blueliner Zach Werenski (ankle, day-to-day per official report) and forward Patrik Laine (out long-term). Home-ice advantage at Bell Centre and Columbus's back-to-back schedule tilt probabilities further, with implied odds showing 68% Habs win likelihood reflecting crowd wisdom on recent form.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their upcoming clash with the Colorado Avalanche, driven by Vegas's superior recent form and home-ice advantage at T-Mobile Arena. The Knights have won four of their last five, bolstered by goaltender Adin Hill's .925 save percentage in recent starts, while Colorado grapples with defensive injuries to Samuel Girard (upper body, out) and a dip in power-play efficiency at 18% over the past 10 games. Nathan MacKinnon's 1.4 points per game remains elite, but Vegas's depth scoring from Mark Stone and William Karlsson exploits Avalanche road woes (3-7 away since March). Watch for line-matchup tweaks and special teams, as these rivals split their season series 2-2.

events.title: Vissel Kōbe vs. Nagoya Grampus - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J1 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vissel Kobe's dominant home record at Noevir Stadium, with four wins in their last five J1 League matches there, anchors trader consensus favoring them against Nagoya Grampus. Kobe sits second in the standings, riding a three-game unbeaten streak, bolstered by Yuya Osako's scoring form (7 goals this season) and no major injuries per official reports. Nagoya, ninth place, struggles away (just two wins in nine), hampered by midfielder Sho Inagaki's suspension risk and recent 2-1 loss to Yokohama F. Marinos. Head-to-head favors Kobe 4-2-1 in last seven, but Nagoya's counterattacks could exploit any fatigue from Kobe's midweek cup tie, keeping markets tight ahead of this pivotal October clash.

events.title: Yokohama F·Marinos vs. FC Tōkyō - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J1 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Yokohama F. Marinos' superior home record at Nissan Stadium drives trader consensus favoring them over FC Tokyo in this J1 League matchup, with markets implying around 55-60% win probability for the hosts. Marinos boast three wins in their last four league games, powered by Anderson Lopes' 15 goals, while dominating recent head-to-heads (4-1 in last five). FC Tokyo's away form falters, winless in five road outings, though Diego Oliveira's scoring threat persists despite a thin squad post-international break. No major injuries reported officially, but Marinos' rest edge after a lighter schedule could prove decisive amid title-chasing momentum versus Tokyo's mid-table push for ACL qualification.
